eliminated_candidates.Graphene: 2D band window (2620–2780 cm⁻¹) is outside scan range; G band is only weak (4.5%); without 2D band confirmation, graphene cannot be identified. The strong peak at 1369 cm⁻¹ is more consistent with hBN E₂g than graphene D band, especially given no G band presence.
eliminated_candidates.hBN: Not eliminated — strong peak at 1369 cm⁻¹ (100% of diagnostic_max) falls squarely in the hBN E₂g window (1355–1380 cm⁻¹); no competing strong peaks from MoS₂, WSe₂, or graphene 2D band to rule it out.
eliminated_candidates.MoS2: MoS₂ diagnostic windows (~383 and ~408 cm⁻¹) are outside the scan range; cannot confirm. No strong peaks in available range consistent with MoS₂.
eliminated_candidates.WSe2: WSe₂ diagnostic windows (~252 cm⁻¹) are outside the scan range; cannot confirm. No evidence of WSe₂ features in available range.
eliminated_candidates.Ti3C2Tx: TMC broad envelope window is outside scan range. The sharp, strong peak at 1369 cm⁻¹ (peak/mean ratio would be high) is inconsistent with the broad, featureless TMC spectrum expected. Sharp crystalline peak argues against TMC.

key_evidence: Strong peak at 1369.0 cm⁻¹ (100% of diagnostic_max) falls within hBN E₂g window (1355–1380 cm⁻¹)
Graphene G band is only weak (4.5% at 1552 cm⁻¹) and 2D band is outside scan range, preventing graphene identification
No other competing strong peaks present in the available scan range (1200–1600 cm⁻¹)
Scan range is limited (1200–1600 cm⁻¹), so MoS₂, WSe₂, and TMC primary diagnostic windows cannot be assessed, but hBN has its primary peak within range
reasoning: The spectrum shows a single dominant sharp peak at 1369 cm⁻¹ (100% of diagnostic_max) that aligns precisely with the hBN E₂g mode (~1366 cm⁻¹). The scan range of 1200–1600 cm⁻¹ covers the hBN diagnostic window fully. Graphene is disfavored because the G band is only weak (4.5%) and the essential 2D band window is outside the scan range, so co-presence of G and 2D cannot be confirmed. Ti₃C₂Tₓ is ruled out by the sharp, dominant crystalline peak. MoS₂ and WSe₂ cannot be confirmed as their primary diagnostic windows lie outside the scan range, but neither produces a dominant single peak near 1369 cm⁻¹. The sharp, strong, isolated peak at ~1369 cm⁻¹ is the hallmark signature of hBN, giving moderate-to-high confidence despite the limited scan range.
